title: Variable update
question: What is the final value of x after this Python code runs?
successMessage: Correct. The second line adds 3 to the previous value of x.
failureMessage: Not quite. Assignment stores the new result back into x, so you must add 2 and 3.

title: Assignment practice
question: What is the value of coins after these lines run?
successMessage: Correct. The old value of coins is increased by 1 and stored back.
failureMessage: Remember that the second line uses the old value first, adds 1, and then stores the result.

title: Predict the output
question: Which value is printed?
successMessage: Exactly. print(name) prints the value stored in the variable.
failureMessage: The variable name is not printed here. Python prints the stored text value instead.

title: Printed number
question: What does this program print?
successMessage: Correct. print(score) outputs the numeric value stored in the variable.
failureMessage: Python prints the stored value, not the variable name itself.

title: Condition check
question: Which operator checks whether two values are equal in Python?
successMessage: Yes. Double equals compares values, while a single equals assigns a value.
failureMessage: Remember: = assigns a value, but == compares whether two values are equal.

title: True or false
question: If lives = 3, will the code inside this if-statement run?
options: No, because 3 is not equal to 1
No, because if only works with strings
successMessage: Correct. The condition lives > 1 is true when lives is 3.
failureMessage: Check the comparison carefully: 3 really is greater than 1, so the block runs.
